mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction and stagflationary pressure, forcing equity valuation multiples to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL increases by greater than 0.5% monthly while RSAFS declines, with a hit rate below 40% over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and output expansion, triggering short-term momentum buying that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, with a directional accuracy below 50% across 10 consecutive recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, breaking the demand-transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly consumer price index increases exceeding 0.6% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.6%, invalidating the tightening transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a directional accuracy below 45% over a 5-year out-of-sample period, rejecting the joint labor-strength premise.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, declining housing starts signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, forecasting lower corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns in late-cycle phases, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across multiple cycle peaks.
